# CGLineCap

**Enumeration**

Styles for rendering the endpoint of a stroked line.

## Declaration

```swift
enum CGLineCap
```

## Overview

A line cap specifies the method used by [doc://com.apple.coregraphics/documentation/CoreGraphics/CGContext/strokePath()] to draw the endpoint of the line. To change the line cap style in a graphics context, you use the function [doc://com.apple.coregraphics/documentation/CoreGraphics/CGContext/setLineCap(_:)].

## Constants

- **CGLineCap.butt**: A line with a squared-off end. Core Graphics draws the line to extend only to the exact endpoint of the path. This is the default.
- **CGLineCap.round**: A line with a rounded end. Core Graphics draws the line to extend beyond the endpoint of the path. The line ends with a semicircular arc with a radius of 1/2 the line’s width, centered on the endpoint.
- **CGLineCap.square**: A line with a squared-off end. Core Graphics extends the line beyond the endpoint of the path for a distance equal to half the line width.

## Initializers

- **init(rawValue:)**

## Conforms To

- BitwiseCopyable
- Equatable
- Hashable
- RawRepresentable
- Sendable
- SendableMetatype
